profile-description:
Actively seeking opportunities in USA.
SME - Data Fabric, Data Catalog & Lineage, Governance, Terraform(IaC), ETL/ELT, AI/ML, RAG, Backend(Python), Frontend(React), CI/CD across AWS, Snowflake, Databricks. Recently graduated from NYU @2026, Former Data & Software Engineering @NBCUniversal.
about-tagline: Give me a spec, I'll build the system that builds the rest!

about-me.para1: Hey — I'm Abhishek. For the last 5 years, I've been building metadata-driven, declarative data platforms that turn multi-terabyte chaos into trusted, business-ready data — with cataloging, lineage, GDPR/CCPA governance, zero-copy sharing, and cost/SLA-aware compute. My toolkit: Spark, Airflow, dbt, AWS, Snowflake, Databricks.
about-me.para2: Currently at NYU, finishing my M.S. in Computer Engineering (May 2026, GPA 3.5) and serving as a Research Assistant — architecting a medallion lakehouse over ~2 TB of MIT SuperCloud HPC telemetry and a two-stage diffusion system to forecast GPU power for datacenter capacity planning. Open to full-time Data Engineer roles in the U.S. for Summer 2026.
about-me.para3: Before that, three years at NBCUniversal as a Data Engineer (Contractor). Architected an enterprise data fabric across AWS, Snowflake, and Databricks — auto-generating pipelines, infrastructure, and orchestration from metadata (~75% faster time-to-market, ~20% infra savings). Processed ~75 TB of ad-impression data, migrated Olympics 2024 reporting to a serverless stack (under 1 hour vs. a 2-hour SLA), and shipped a schema-grounded RAG assistant for text-to-SQL and document Q&A.
about-me.para4: I also extend into generative modeling, RAG, lakehouses, and vector databases — from credit-risk analytics on Databricks to decade-scale NYC housing market pipelines. I care about clean abstractions, policy-enforced self-service, and pipelines that don't wake you up at 3 AM.

exp1.name: New York University@https://www.nyu.edu/
exp1.comment:
HPC Telemetry Lakehouse & Power-Forecasting Pipeline
(GCP, PySpark, PyTorch, Parquet)

exp4.name: NBCUniversal@https://www.nbcuniversal.com/
exp4.comment:
Enterprise Data Fabric · Olympics 2024 ETL · Schema-Grounded RAG
(AWS, Snowflake, Databricks, Airflow, Terraform)

1.name: Credit Risk GPT
1.description: Credit-risk analytics platform over ~2.26M Lending Club loans — a governed Databricks lakehouse (Lakeflow Declarative Pipelines, Unity Catalog lineage/RBAC) materializing risk KPIs by grade, vintage, geography, and DTI. Extended with a RAG analyst assistant grounding credit-risk Q&A in CFPB regulatory complaint narratives alongside governed metrics — surfacing consumer-protection and compliance signals raw loan data alone misses.

2.name: HPC Telemetry Lakehouse & Power-Forecasting Pipeline
2.description: NYU research: medallion lakehouse over ~2 TB of MIT SuperCloud telemetry — 460K+ compute jobs (98K GPU), V100 GPUs, 9 months — with AQE, partitioning, and skew handling on a 16-node cluster (~80% latency cut). A two-stage diffusion system synthesizes SLURM job metadata and generates conditioned GPU power traces (peak Corr = 0.93) for datacenter power forecasting and capacity planning.

3.name: Enterprise Data Fabric
3.description: NBCUniversal declarative data platform: a SQL spec auto-provisions ingestion, transformation, orchestration, and IaC from metadata — collapsing new-pipeline delivery from weeks to hours (~75% faster). Graph-modeled catalog with cross-domain ontologies, automated lineage, GDPR/CCPA RBAC, SLA-driven compute across Lambda/EMR/Databricks/Snowflake (~20% cost savings), and zero-copy sharing — processing ~75 TB of ad-impression data with full observability.

4.name: Olympics 2024 Serverless ETL Migration
4.description: Migrated Olympics 2024 ad-impression reporting from on-prem Cassandra and on-demand Databricks to a fully serverless AWS stack under a hard deadline. Re-architected fragmented legacy jobs into a config-driven Airflow framework with parallelized Spark and Lambda tasks — sustaining twice-daily full refresh of 60+ datasets and cutting end-to-end runtime from ~2 hours to under 1 hour (~50%), clearing the 2-hour SLA with headroom.

5.name: Schema-Grounded Enterprise Retrieval (RAG)
5.description: Schema-grounded RAG assistant (React/Flask, CI/CD) unifying text-to-SQL and document Q&A by retrieving over a graph metadata catalog (KeyBERT-indexed) — grounding SQL to real tables/columns and citation-backed answers from docs, wikis, and PDFs. SQL validation before execution and event-driven metadata refresh counter knowledge drift — one RBAC-governed interface cutting analyst self-service from ~30 minutes to under a minute.

6.name: NYC Housing Pulse (2015–2025)
6.description: S3-backed batch pipeline ingesting ~900K NYC Dept. of Finance property-sale transactions across all 5 boroughs (2015–2025) — resolving inconsistent schemas, malformed addresses, and duplicate parcels into a clean serving layer. Borough- and year-level analytics (price-per-sqft, sales volume, affordability trends) power a QuickSight dashboard tracing a decade of market shifts across neighborhoods and price tiers.

7.name: Abhishek Agrawal's Portfolio
7.description: This site — React SPA with a custom dark palette, glass surfaces, conic-gradient portrait ring, alternating experience timeline, ambient gradients, and a FormSubmit-backed contact flow. Tuned for fast first paint and readable long-form engineering narrative.

8.name: Churn Prediction & Incentive Engine
8.description: End-to-end AWS pattern for churn: curated features in S3, SageMaker training and batch scoring, DynamoDB for low-latency scores, and Lambda functions driving a real-time incentive and segmentation service for lifecycle campaigns and experiments.
